message.content:
<deliberation>
I will evaluate each main GDPR node against the provided policy text. I will treat any missing elements as non-deviations per the instructions and only flag deviations where the policy text expressly narrows, broadens, negates, or otherwise changes the requirement. I will also list and consider any cross-references explicitly mentioned in each node using the provided referenced_nodes. Only REG-092 presents an explicit scope change (“this Regulation” vs. “data protection law”), which I classify as a severity deviation (over-broadening the condition for complaint). REG-093 and REG-095 are simply not addressed by the policy text and therefore count as non-deviations under the omission rule.
</deliberation>
```json
[
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-092",
    "clause": "Art77(1)",
    "considered_references": [],
    "deviation_found": true,
    "deviations": [
      {
        "deviation_type": "Severity deviation",
        "gdpr_quote": "if the data subject considers that the processing of personal data relating to him or her infringes this Regulation.",
        "policy_quote": "if you believe that the processing of your personal data infringes data protection law.",
        "mismatch": "The policy broadens the condition for lodging a complaint from infringements of “this Regulation” (GDPR) to infringements of generic “data protection law.”",
        "analysis": "GDPR Art 77(1) ties the complaint right to infringements of the GDPR specifically, whereas the policy extends it to any data protection law, making it more permissive than required."
      }
    ]
  },
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-093",
    "clause": "Art77(2)",
    "considered_references": ["REG-094", "REG-095", "REG-096", "REG-097"],
    "deviation_found": false,
    "deviations": []
  },
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-095",
    "clause": "Art78(2)",
    "considered_references": ["REG-092", "REG-093"],
    "deviation_found": false,
    "deviations": []
  }
]
```
